samples.xml_description:
<module id="freq_div">
    <description>Divide the input frequency of 100MHz signal, and the outputs are 3 clock frequencies: 50MHz, 10MHz, 1MHz.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK_in">Input clock signal</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal</input>
        <output id="CLK_50">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2.</output>
        <output id="CLK_10">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10.</output>
        <output id="CLK_1">Output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="CLK_in">The input clock signal</parameter>
        <parameter id="RST">Reset signal</parameter>
        <parameter id="CLK_50">The output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2</parameter>
        <parameter id="CLK_10">The output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10</parameter>
        <parameter id="CLK_1">The output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="CLK_50">
            <description>Reset the output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2, to 0 when the reset signal is active.</description>
            <statement>
                <combinational_logic id="CLK_50">
                    <description>Set the output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2, to 0 when the reset signal is active.</description>
                    <statement>
                        <condition>
                            <true>
                                <assignment>
                                    <simple_assignment>
                                        <left><input id="CLK_50"/></left>
                                        <right>0</right>
                                    </simple_assignment>
                                </assignment>
                            </true>
                        </condition>
                    </statement>
                </combinational_logic>
            </statement>
            <description>On every positive edge of CLK_in or RST, if RST is active, CLK_50 is set to 0.</description>
            <statement>
                <condition>
                    <or>
                        <true><event><input id="CLK_in"/> rising_edge</event></true>
                        <true><input id="RST"/> rising_edge</true>
                    </or>
                    <assignment>
                        <simple_assignment>
                            <left><input id="CLK_50"/></left>
                            <right><input id="CLK_50"/> ~<input id="CLK_50"/></right>
                        </simple_assignment>
                    </assignment>
                </condition>
            </statement>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="CLK_10">
            <description>Reset the output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10, to 0 when the reset signal is active.</description>
            <statement>
                <condition>
                    <true>
                        <assignment>
                            <simple_assignment>
                                <left><input id="CLK_10"/></left>
                                <right>0</right>
                            </simple_assignment>
                        </assignment>
                    </true>
                </condition>
            </statement>
            The output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10, is toggled when the counter cnt_10 is 4.
            <statement>
                <condition>
                    <true><event><input id="CLK_in"/> rising_edge</event></true>
                    <true><input id="RST"/> rising_edge</true>
                </condition>
                <assignment>
                    <simple_assignment>
                        <left><input id="CLK_10"/></left>
                        <right><input id="CLK_10"/> ~<input id="CLK_10"/></right>
                    </simple_assignment>
                </assignment>
            </statement>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="CLK_1">
            <description>Reset the output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100, to 0 when the reset signal is active.</description>
            <statement>
                <condition>
                    <true>
                        <assignment>
                            <simple_assignment>
                                <left><input id="CLK_1"/></left>
                                <right>0</right>
                            </simple_assignment>
                        </assignment>
                    </true>
                </condition>
            </statement>
            The output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100, is toggled when the counter cnt_100 is 49.
            <statement>
                <condition>
                    <true><event><input id="CLK_in"/> rising_edge</event></true>
                    <true><input id="RST"/> rising_edge</true>
                </condition>
                <assignment>
                    <simple_assignment>
                        <left><input id="CLK_1"/></left>
                        <right><input id="CLK_1"/> ~<input id="CLK_1"/></right>
                    </simple_assignment>
                </assignment>
            </statement>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Reset the output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 2, to 0 when the reset signal is active. On every positive edge of CLK_in or RST, if RST is active, CLK_50 is set to 0. Otherwise, CLK_50 is toggled by inverting its current value. Reset the output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10, to 0 when the reset signal is active. The output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 10, is toggled when the counter cnt_10 is 4. Reset the output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100, to 0 when the reset signal is active. The output clock signal with a frequency of CLK_in divided by 100, is toggled when the counter cnt_100 is 49.</implementation>
</module>
